steps.title: Energy is never lost
steps.body:
Energy doesn't appear or disappear — it just changes form. A roller coaster at the top of the hill is all **potential energy**; at the bottom it's all **kinetic**. The total never changed.

This is the big idea of the whole unit.

steps.title: The rule
steps.body: When only **conservative** forces (like gravity or springs) act, mechanical energy stays constant — KE and U just trade back and forth.

steps.title: Get a feel for it
steps.body: Slide the ball down the frictionless ramp. Watch potential energy pour into kinetic energy — while the total bar never moves.

steps.prompt: A ball is released from rest at the top of a frictionless ramp. At the bottom, compared to the top, it has…
choices: The same total energy, now all kinetic
More energy than it started with
hints: No friction is the key here — what does that mean for the total mechanical energy?
The energy isn't created or destroyed: the U_g it gives up reappears as KE. Same total, just a new form.

steps.title: Work through one
steps.body:
A **2 kg** block starts at rest at **h = 2 m** on a frictionless ramp.

At the top: U_g = mgh = 2 × 9.8 × 2 = 39.2 J, KE = 0.
At the bottom: all of it is kinetic → KE = **39.2 J**.

Your turn.

steps.prompt: A **2 kg** block starts at rest at **h = 2 m** and slides down a **frictionless** ramp. Drag the **KE** at the bottom.
hints: No friction means the U_g it loses becomes KE it gains.
Find the U_g at the top with m × g × h — that whole amount shows up as KE at the bottom.

steps.title: When friction joins in
steps.body: Friction doesn't destroy energy either — it turns mechanical energy into **thermal energy (E_th)**, which heats things up. So now the books balance like this:

steps.prompt: Same drop, but friction made **E_th = 19.6 J**. Starting from **U_g = 39.2 J**, drag the **KE** left at the bottom.
hints: Friction turns some energy into heat, so the books balance as U_g = KE + E_th.
Rearrange to KE = U_g − E_th, then subtract the thermal energy from the starting U_g.

steps.title: Lesson complete!
steps.body: You balanced energy with and without friction — conserved when it applies, turned to heat when friction acts.
